purpose: Explicit TLS boundary map. Declares allowed responsibilities, forbidden responsibilities, allowed artifact families, forbidden artifact name patterns, safe vocabulary, and owner handoff rules. TLS remains recommendation-only / observation-only / owner-input only.
tls_role_statement: TLS produces ranking, classification, evidence-attached observations, trust-gap signals, recommendation packets, and owner-input packets. TLS does not own runtime, gating, or any owner outcome. CDE, TPA, SEL, GOV, and HIT retain canonical ownership of control, policy, enforcement, governance, and human-in-the-loop outcomes.

allowed_responsibilities.name: registry_parsing
allowed_responsibilities.description: Parse the canonical system_registry.md into a deterministic dependency graph artifact (system_registry_dependency_graph.json).

allowed_responsibilities.name: evidence_observation
allowed_responsibilities.description: Attach observed evidence (file presence, lineage refs, replay refs) to system nodes in evidence-attachment artifacts.

allowed_responsibilities.name: candidate_classification
allowed_responsibilities.description: Classify systems into candidate categories (do-not-touch, candidate, blocked) without owning any owner outcome.

allowed_responsibilities.name: trust_gap_signal_reporting
allowed_responsibilities.description: Report trust-gap signals over governed evidence; signals are inputs, not outcomes.

allowed_responsibilities.name: priority_ranking
allowed_responsibilities.description: Compute deterministic priority ranking from governed inputs and write a ranking report artifact.

allowed_responsibilities.name: requested_candidate_ranking
allowed_responsibilities.description: Generate operator-requested candidate ranking lists from governed evidence; ordering is preserved by downstream consumers.

allowed_responsibilities.name: explanation_generation
allowed_responsibilities.description: Emit human-readable explanations for ranking, classification, and trust-gap signals; explanations are observations, never outcomes.

allowed_responsibilities.name: roadmap_backed_leverage_queue
allowed_responsibilities.description: Group governed roadmap safe_bundles into a leverage queue artifact; ordering is artifact-derived only.

allowed_responsibilities.name: owner_input_packet_generation_only
allowed_responsibilities.description: Emit owner-input packets that downstream canonical owners (CDE / TPA / SEL / GOV / HIT) consume; TLS does not emit owner outcomes.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: control_outcomes
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Control outcomes are owned by CDE/TPA. TLS may emit control_input observations only.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: owner_approvals
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Owner approvals are owned by GOV/HIT. TLS may emit review_request signals only.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: enforcement_actions
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Enforcement actions are owned by SEL. TLS may emit enforcement_signal observations only.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: certification_outcomes
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Certification outcomes are owned by CDE/GOV. TLS may emit certification_input evidence only.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: promotion_readiness_outcomes
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Promotion readiness outcomes are owned by CDE/GOV. TLS may emit readiness_signal observations only.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: authority_decisions
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Authority outcomes are reserved by the authority registry. TLS may emit authority_signal observations only.

forbidden_responsibilities.name: runtime_execution_mutation
forbidden_responsibilities.rationale: Runtime mutation is owned by PQX/AEX/SEL. TLS produces no runtime side effects of any kind.

owner_handoff_rules.statement: TLS may emit owner-input packets for downstream review.
CDE, TPA, SEL, GOV, and HIT remain canonical owners of control-outcome, enforcement-outcome, certification-outcome, promotion-readiness-outcome, and approval-outcome artifacts.
TLS may not emit owner outcomes. TLS only provides inputs, signals, observations, recommendations, and owner-input packets.
Every TLS owner-input packet must declare recommendation_only=true and owner_outcome_present=false.

stop_conditions: All TLS-owned interfaces classified as allowed or forbidden.
Every allowed responsibility maps to one allowed artifact family.
Every forbidden responsibility maps to a canonical owner.
All TLS owner-input packets carry recommendation_only=true.
